text.title: cognitive-walkthrough Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for cognitive-walkthrough as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLM agent answers "Yes" to Q2 and Q3 without positive evidence from the screenshot or DOM.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents default to charitable interpretation; require "Yes only if the screenshot shows X" in the prompt to force grounding.

failure-mode.description: Agent conflates Q1 (does user know they need to act?) with Q3 (does label match the action?).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: These are distinct failure types—Q1 is motivational, Q3 is labelling—separate prompts prevent cross-contamination.

failure-mode.description: Agent answers Q4 for the page transition rather than for the user's stated goal.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A page load is not progress feedback; the user's internal narrative ("did I complete step X?") is the correct reference.

failure-mode.description: Persona file not reseeded each step; agent forgets the persona midway through a long session.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs drift from persona context over long conversations; reseed the persona description at each step.

failure-mode.description: Agent marks all issues as High severity.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Severity inflation renders prioritization meaningless; apply the H/M/L rubric explicitly: H = blocks task, M = extra turn, L = polish.

failure-mode.description: Evaluators bring tacit knowledge—"obvious" buttons aren't obvious to the persona.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Product familiarity is the largest source of evaluator bias. Explicitly bracket this knowledge and stick to the persona.

failure-mode.description: Group-think when 2-4 evaluators converge fast on the first answer; structure each question independently.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Social dynamics lead to premature consensus. Independent answering and simultaneous reveal prevents this.

failure-mode.description: Misses motivational issues ("user knows what to do, just does not want to").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CW is a learnability method, not a preference or value method. If motivation is the failure mode, use different research.

failure-mode.description: Ignores accessibility unless persona explicitly includes AT use.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CW evaluates learnability, not accessibility. Pair with an accessibility audit (axe, pa11y) to catch WCAG issues.
